weather_report:
Late morning in Subiaco feels muted, the sun softened behind a pale lid of cloud. Through the afternoon the cover is likely to thicken into a steadier overcast before loosening toward evening into broken, shifting patches. Brightness will come in shy, silvery pulses rather than full sunshine, and the UV sits on the gentle side for winter—nothing fierce, but worth a dab of sunscreen if you linger outside when the sky briefly opens. The air looks clear enough, yet the light stays flat and calm, the sort that hushes the day rather than spurring it on.

It reads as a cool, unhurried day: pleasant for errands and a slow circuit of the local shops, or a coffee by the window where passing clouds edit the afternoon. Dress in easy layers—a soft sweater and a light jacket you can shrug off if a brighter spell arrives—and comfortable shoes. If you want a little motion, wait for a gap in the cloud and take a measured stroll through a nearby park, letting the muted skyline set the tempo.
